author: @tmjones7081
text:
Luke, I had an idea the other day! 
When I was building my little turbine
generator, I wondered what if I added small magnets at the tips or somewhere along the blades? Then build a frame that held 1 or 2 transformers close enough to generate more power? 

Is this really even an added benefit? Or any issues with this idea that im missing? 

Congrats! And thanks!

author: @timothygorman7528
text: I'll really miss you if you leave FWG while going to school.  I've always enjoyed your videos and your creativity.  Engineering, and learning how things actually work will be a great and useful tool for you.  From the time I was a boy, I wanted to fly planes, and not just any planes, I wanted specifically to fly airliners.  I went to a University in Texas (I'm from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania) and got a B.S. Degree in Aviation Technology.  This particular program was designed to make better pilots by teaching them the mechanics as well as the flight.  You graduated with all three Airplane Mechanic Ratings as well as all of your flight ratings.  The first week into "Intro to Aviation,"  I was scared to death.  Although I could change the oil, brakes and spark plugs in my car, my Public Education had always been a "College Prep" kind of path, and no one had ever taught me basic Engine theory.  I knew they were in there, but I couldn't tell you the difference between a piston and cylinder, or how a 4-cycle or 2-cycle basic engine worked.  Even though I never wanted to be a mechanic, the information and experience I gained in that first two years has come in so handy in my life, even with just my own cars!  And also in just learning the basics of how things work! Good luck to you there - remember, some of the classes are going to seem boring, and others will be seriously challenging.  College is nothing like grade school - people in college usually want to be there.  Take all the advantage of it you can.

author: @totherarf
text:
There are 2 ways to get a degree.

1. Remember the stuff they will teach you.

2. Understand the stuff they teach you .... and how it evolved to become what it is.

The world is full of number 1's
Number 2 is the way to excel and have fun doing it. ..... and maybe pass that knowledge on to others!

author: @cirdiam1800
text:
Keep in mind a Venn diagram of 3 circles: 1. What you like to do, 2. what you are good at, 3. What pays.  
Find things that fit in the overlap of all 3 circles. Pursue one of those.
